title: Când anti-imperialismul sufocă umanismul
summary: The article criticizes the Romanian left's ideological contradiction of opposing Western imperialism while ignoring human rights abuses by anti-Western authoritarian regimes like Iran, Russia, and Hezbollah. The author argues that this selective anti-imperialism leads to a cynical triumph of ideology over humanist values, as leftists stay silent on Iranian protesters being killed, Ukrainian civilians being massacred, and women's oppression under theocratic regimes.
talking_points: The Romanian left faces a false dilemma between consistent anti-imperialism and supporting Iranian protesters against the mullah regime
Deaths and human rights abuses become ideologically inconvenient when perpetrated by regimes considered allies against Western imperialism
Self-declared feminist leftists close their eyes to crimes of deeply misogynistic regimes like Iran to avoid aiding American imperialism
The geopolitical instrumentalization of Iran's situation represents the cynical triumph of ideology over humanist values
The left's selective anti-imperialism blocks progress precisely in authoritarian non-Western zones where it is most needed
concepts: enemy of my enemy alliance logic
Western vs. Russian imperialism double standard
related_concepts: human rights universalism vs. ideological selectivity
author: Doina Popescu
